title: Other Revenues: Investments
description: Conroe ISD follows specific rules for investing public funds — such as tax revenues and fees — to keep that money safe and growing until it is needed for school operations. The district is required to prioritize security and liquidity over maximizing returns, ensuring funds are available when needed for students and staff. These guidelines help protect taxpayer dollars and ensure the community's money is managed responsibly and transparently.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's CDA (Local) investment policy adds two new requirements: (1) a statement that District funds shall be invested in accordance with the Public Funds Investment Act, and (2) a requirement that distributors of investment pools be registered with the Texas State Securities Board, SIPC/FINRA, and also be a registrant in good standing with the Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB).
changes_added: Funds of the District shall be invested in accordance with the Public Funds Investment Act.
and of distributors of investment pools
Distributors of investment pools shall also be a registrant in good standing with the Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB).
timeline_local.notes: Red underlined text was clearly identifiable on pages 3 and 5. No red strikethrough (removed) text was found in the document. The addition on page 5 inserts "and of distributors of investment pools" into an existing sentence about broker/dealer registration requirements, and adds a new sentence requiring MSRB registration for distributors of investment pools.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's CDA (Local) Investment Policy adds a sentence requiring District funds to be invested in accordance with the Public Funds Investment Act, and expands broker/dealer registration requirements to include distributors of investment pools, who must also be registered with the Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB).
changes_added: Funds of the District shall be invested in accordance with the Public Funds Investment Act.
and of distributors of investment pools
Distributors of investment pools shall also be a registrant in good standing with the Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB).
timeline_local.notes: Red underlined text was clearly identifiable on pages 3 and 5. No red strikethrough (removal) text was detected anywhere in the redline document. The cover page title reads "Discuss Revisions to Board Policy CDA (Local)" indicating a first reading.

description_local: This policy governs how the school district detects, prevents, and reports fraud, with a specific focus on disclosing required information when the district applies for or receives federal grant funding. It applies to district administrators and staff who manage federal awards, but it also affects the broader community since federal funds support many school programs and services. Parents and community members should care about this policy because it helps ensure that taxpayer and federal dollars are used honestly and transparently, and that the district meets the legal requirements tied to receiving federal funding.
